rulings.Case Title: ROGELIO HECHAVARRIA VS. ELIAS GEORGOPOULOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Further Responses Ato Form Interrogatories, And Suppleemntal Priviledge Log
rulings.Rulings: On Calendar Monday, December 12, 2011, Line 6. DEFENDANT ELIAS GEORGOPOULOS' Motion To Compel Further Respos. To Special Rogs, Further Responses to Form Rogs, And Supp. Priviledge Log. Granted in part as to special interrogatory 1. Plaintiff shall provide further responses but need not include facilities where pliantiff received mental health care. Granted as to special Rogs. 9, 15, and 19. These requests seek relevant information. Granted in part as to form Rog. 12.1 and special Rogs. 12. Plaintiff need not identify every individual who may have knowledge of the incident, but shall provide a list of witnesses to the incident known to him. Denied as to special interrogatories 23 and 24. These requests are no longer relevant. Denied as to special interrogatories 33 and 34. Plaintiff provided all of the responsive information that he has. Denied as to form interrogatory 12.2. A list of witness interviewed by counsel is privileged because it ¿would necessarily reflect counsel¿s evaluation of the case by revealing which witnesses or persons who claimed knowledge of the incident counsel deemed important enough to interview.¿ (Nacht & Lewis Architects, Inc. v. Superior Cour, (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 214, 217). Granted as to the privilege log. The purpose of a privilege log is to provide a factual description of documents substantiating a claim of privilege and to aid the Court in identifying whether there is a privilege. A privilege log that fails to identify specific documents is meaningless. Requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHK

rulings.Case Title: TOKUNBO SOLARIN VS. JMDH REAL ESTATE OF SAN FRANCISCO II LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Stay Pending Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2011, Line 10. DEFENDANT JMDH REAL ESTATE OF SAN FRANCISCO II LLC Motion For Stay Pending Appeal. Granted. The court has inherent authority under Code of Civil Procedure 128(a)(3) and (8) to control the conduct of the parties and the proceedings before it. This motion is not a motion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure 1008 and was properly set in this department. Staying this action pending a final decision by the Appellate Division in case number 634591 promotes a just and efficient resolution.
